mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is elevated, equity discount rates stay restrictive and 20-day S&P 500 returns are biased negative; the signal is strongest after a recent rise in policy rates because tighter financial conditions compress valuation multiples.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.06% in the latest snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS stays above 4.5%, or if the rolling 2-year rank IC is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in UNRATE above the prior 3-month average signals weakening household income and rising recession risk, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month through lower expected earnings and higher risk aversion.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp above its 3-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% in the latest snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative 20-day returns on average, or if positive returns occur in at least 60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS accelerates while HOUST stays weak, consumer spending is holding up despite fragile housing demand; this mix supports short-run revenue expectations and should lift the S&P 500 over the next week.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS and HOUST are both available at monthly frequency
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term does not outperform either single-series proxy, or if the triggered basket of dates has negative average 5-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: An improvement in INDPRO after a prior decline indicates factory output recovery, implying better near-term earnings momentum for cyclicals and a positive S&P 500 response over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.0107 in the latest snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if rising INDPRO does not coincide with positive average 20-day returns, or if the signal turns non-positive in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in GDPC1 implies broad final demand contraction and lower expected corporate sales, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next quarter as analysts revise earnings downward.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 is observed only quarterly and lags the real-time snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if quarters with negative GDPC1 growth are followed by positive 60-day equity returns more often than not, or if the signal has no negative edge versus baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS falls while UNRATE rises, the labor market is deteriorating in a way that should quickly weaken earnings expectations and raise risk premia, producing negative S&P 500 returns within 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS and UNRATE are both monthly labor indicators
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor stress condition does not produce negative 20-day returns on average, or if a single-series PAYEMS or UNRATE rule explains the result equally well.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary regime, a falling CPIAUCSL combined with unchanged FEDFUNDS implies a higher real policy rate and tighter effective financial conditions, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation rate falls below 0 while FEDFUNDS is unchanged or rising
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 303.294 and FEDFUNDS at 5.06 in the latest snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction does not predict negative 20-day returns better than CPIAUCSL alone, or if triggered periods show neutral/positive equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST after prior weakness signals a housing-led demand recession, and equities should sell off sharply over the next week as investors price lower construction activity, credit demand, and household wealth effects.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 5% month over month and remains below its 6-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1401.0 in the latest snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if sharp HOUST declines are not followed by negative 5-day returns on average, or if the hit rate is below 40% in triggered observations.
